pages.text: $(italic)Waystones$() are the pack's long-distance travel. A $(bold)Waystone$() is a waist-high pillar you place and name; once you've touched it, you can warp back to it from any other waystone — and, with a $(italic)Warp Stone$() or the inventory button, from anywhere at all.$(br2)You'll also find them already standing in villages and out in the wild, waiting to be activated as you pass.
pages.title: Waystones

pages.text: $(bold)The cost is experience.$() A warp spends levels by distance — roughly $(bold)one level per 1000 blocks$() travelled. Short hops are nearly free; crossing the world costs real XP. There is no fuel and no item to carry; the price is paid from your bar.$(br2)$(li)$(bold)Warp Stone$() — a carried, rechargeable warp; about a $(bold)30-second$() cooldown.$(li)$(bold)Inventory button$() — warp to a global waystone from your inventory screen; a longer ($(bold)5-minute$()) cooldown.
pages.title: The Cost Is Experience

pages.text: $(bold)Global waystones.$() A waystone can be set $(italic)global$() — then it appears in every player's list for free, the backbone of a shared map. On the Novus server these knit the world together: a global hub at spawn, and your own private stones for home.$(br2)$(italic)Activating a stone is permanent; naming it well is the only real skill.$()
pages.title: Global Waystones
